nuggets.headline: Pete Rango has engineered live sound for A$AP Ferg, XXXTentacion, and David Duchovny
nuggets.text: Beyond his own productions, Rango has spent years as a freelance live audio engineer for a startlingly diverse roster of acts. His resume includes hip-hop heavyweights like Denzel Curry and Kodak Black, R&B artists, and even actor David Duchovny, showcasing a technical versatility that underpins his studio work.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I have been freelancing in the Audio/Visual world for over 4 years doing live audio for concerts (Dom Kennedy, DJ Mustard, ASAP Ferg... Denzel Curry, XXXTentacion, Kodak Black... David Duchovny), as well as conferences for Facebook, Instagram, Visa...

nuggets.imageCaption: Producer and engineer Pete Rango working in his studio.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango runs a nonprofit creative lab called L.I.V. (Life is Valuable)
nuggets.text: Before committing to music, Rango intended to study child developmental psychology. He now channels that impulse into L.I.V., a nonprofit platform focused on artist development, youth mentorship, and storytelling that bridges education with creative work and social impact.

source.title: Pete Rango
source.quoteSnippet: L.I.V. (Life is Valuable) ... A nonprofit creative lab and community platform. Focused on artist development, youth mentorship, and storytelling through music, tech, and culture.

nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: for a similar production approach, listen to Pete Rango's collaborator Abjo
nuggets.text: If you're drawn to the textures in Rango's work, explore the catalog of producer Abjo. The two share a production credit, alongside Hudson Mohawke, on the track "Tomorrow" from Jesse Boykins III's EP *Bartholomew WAVE I*. Abjo's work with the Soulection collective carries a similar sensibility; start with his album *Sankofa*.

source.title: Meet Pete Rango - Voyage MIA Magazine | Miami City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: That’s when we ended up landing a song on Jesse Boykins III EP Bartholomew WAVE I with Hudson Mowhake and Abjo.

nuggets.imageCaption: Producer Abjo, who collaborated with Pete Rango on a Jesse Boykins III track.

artistSummary: Bogota-born producer Pete Rango channels a lifetime of diverse influences—from fleeing persecution in Colombia to playing in metal bands and engineering live sound for hip-hop's biggest names. Now a creative director and founder of the nonprofit L.I.V., his work is a fusion of artist development, cultural curiosity, and technical skill.
